response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically broad engineer with 16+ years of experience across distributed systems, full-stack development, and embedded work, holding Staff-level titles at multiple companies. However, the Principal Growth/Experimentation Systems role requires deep, demonstrable expertise in building experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature-flagging/rollout systems, telemetry infrastructure, and growth measurement pipelines — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His closest adjacent signal is the high-throughput micro-services work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second), but that was low-latency data integration, not experimentation or growth. The domain gap is too significant to bridge at IC6 Principal level, where the expectation is to own and lead this entire engineering surface from day one.
strengths: Polyglot with 16+ years spanning JavaScript/TypeScript, C++, C#, Java, and DLang, satisfying the language breadth requirement on paper.
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second), showing production-scale engineering fundamentals.
Community leadership track record via conference talks, multi-day workshops, and open source library authorship, indicating ability to influence beyond immediate team.
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience building experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature-flagging systems, or statistically sound rollout pipelines — the core of this role.
No evidence of telemetry/instrumentation platform ownership, growth metrics systems, or data-driven experimentation analysis at any employer.
Current seniority and scope profile (generalist Senior/Staff) falls short of the cross-team Principal IC6 mandate; no history of owning multi-team architectural initiatives in this domain.

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or growth measurement background
